remember: Students will be able to recall the structure and composition of the Indian judiciary.
Students will be able to identify the various courts in the Indian judiciary.
Students will be able to list the key features of the Indian judiciary.
understand: Students will be able to explain the role and functions of the judiciary in the Indian Constitution.
Students will be able to describe the process of judicial appointments in India.
Students will be able to analyze the relationship between the judiciary and the other branches of government.
apply: Students will be able to apply the principles of judicial independence in a given scenario.
Students will be able to use the concepts of judicial review and the separation of powers to evaluate a case.
Students will be able to analyze the impact of judicial decisions on society.
analyze: Students will be able to analyze the strengths and weaknesses of the Indian judiciary.
Students will be able to evaluate the effectiveness of the judicial system in India.
Students will be able to compare and contrast the Indian judiciary with other countries' judicial systems.

ncert_objectives: As per NCERT, students will be able to understand the role of the judiciary in upholding the Constitution and the rule of law.
Students will be able to describe the evolution of the Indian judiciary from the British period to the present day.
Students will be able to analyze the key cases that have shaped the Indian judiciary.
board_exam_objectives: Students will be able to explain the structure and composition of the Indian judiciary (CBSE Class 11 Political Science Question 1).
Students will be able to describe the role and functions of the judiciary in the Indian Constitution (CBSE Class 11 Political Science Question 2).
Students will be able to analyze the impact of judicial decisions on society (CBSE Class 11 Political Science Question 3).
